pages.text: Reactors are custom multiblocks. A valid reactor needs a $(l:unnamednuclear:reactor_casing)Casing$ and a $(l:unnamednuclear:reactor_controller)Controller$. $(br2)Inside the casing, you place $(l:unnamednuclear:fuel_channel)Fuel Channels$, $(l:unnamednuclear:moderator)Moderators$, and $(l:unnamednuclear:control_channel)Control Channels$.

Reactors are 3D grid-based. Each block interacts with its neighbors through neutrons. $(br2)Fast Neutrons: Emitted by fission, need moderation.$(br)Thermal Neutrons: Slowed down by moderators, high probability of causing fission in U-235.

Safety is critical. $(br2)Control Rods: Absorb neutrons to slow the reaction.$(br)Xenon-135: A byproduct of fission that poisons the reaction. $(br)Meltdowns: If heat exceeds 2000 units, the core will explode!

The $(l:unnamednuclear:debug_item)Nuclear Analysis Tool$ can be used to visualize neutron flux and heat in real-time. Holding it will highlight active simulation nodes with colored boxes.$(br)Red: Heat$(br)Green: Thermal Neutrons$(br)Blue: Fast Neutrons
Slows down neutrons to increase fission efficiency.
